Function to suspend a thread for a specified number of milliseconds in firmware or bootloader.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that declares a function [`psleep`](<#psleep>), which is used to pause execution for a specified number of milliseconds. The function can either suspend the thread in firmware or perform a busy wait in the bootloader. The documentation notes that the actual sleep duration may be less than expected if the current tick is already in progress when the function is called. Additionally, the thread may not resume immediately after the sleep period, potentially resulting in a longer wait time. The function takes a single parameter, `millis`, which specifies the number of milliseconds to wait.
